attacking_style: Commits to long-range breakaways spanning multiple days, prioritizing classification points over stage wins. Lacks selectivity in break composition, often ending in low-reward efforts despite high energy expenditure.
team_tactics: Team deploys rider for extended TV exposure through breakaway participation, accumulating 800km+ breaks across multi-stage periods. Classification hunting appears secondary to sponsor visibility objectives.
terrain_preference: Mountainous stages where KOM points are available. Capable of sustaining multi-day breakaway efforts but lacks punch for stage victories from these moves.
vulnerability: Weak break selection judgment leads to low-percentage efforts. Unable to convert KOM jersey opportunities into meaningful points hauls, suggesting poor tactical reading of competition dynamics.
key_patterns: Participates in extremely long breakaways (800km accumulated) prioritizing visibility over results (2 observations)
Targets KOM classification from breaks but achieves minimal points return despite high effort investment (2 observations)
Multi-day breakaway commitment pattern suggests team directive rather than rider-initiated tactics (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Consistent breakaway role across 2018-2019 Giro appearances suggests stable domestique function rather than developing threat
